free_text: Level structure of Tc-99 by inelastic scattering and
proton stripping.
AVF cyclotron was used.
Particle identification was performed
by measuring the energy loss of the particles in a
plastic scintillator located behind a position-
sensitive helical-cathode proportional counter
positioned in the focal plane of the spectrograph.
The absolute normalization of the scattering data was
obtained by comparing the elastic scattering data to
the predictions of an optical model calculation at
small angles.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
The Tc-99 target, approximately 50 mu-g/cm2 thick,
was in the form of the pure radioactive isotope
evaporated onto a carbon foil.
Fig.2 from Nucl.Phys.,A290(1977)155
SD: ERR-DIG value corrected (0.015 -> 1.5%)
The accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
The Mo-98 target was in the form of the highly enriched
oxide evaporated onto a thin carbon foil and was about
50 mu-g/cm2 in thickness.
